findings.title: Classic Reentrancy in withdraw() - External Call Before State Update
findings.reason: The withdraw() function executes msg.sender.call{value: amount}("") at line 26 before updating balances[msg.sender] -= amount at line 30, enabling recursive withdrawal exploitation.

findings.title: Missing Event Emission Protection
findings.reason: The Withdrawal event is emitted after state update but within the reentrancy-vulnerable function, causing incorrect event logs during recursive calls.
